tabs.title: Company Overview
tabs.content:
**HelioPay Financial** is a fintech startup building a next-generation payment processing platform for small and medium-sized businesses. The platform enables merchants to accept credit cards, digital wallets, and ACH transfers through a unified API.

The company is in the process of obtaining Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) Level 1 certification and expects to process $500 million in transactions annually within the first year of operation. HelioPay is building its infrastructure entirely on AWS to leverage cloud security controls and achieve rapid time-to-market.

The founding team includes experienced engineers from major payment processors, and they are committed to building a highly secure, compliant, and resilient platform from day one.

tabs.title: Planned Architecture
tabs.content:
**Application Components:**
- **Public-facing API**: RESTful payment processing API accessible to merchant applications
- **Admin Dashboard**: Web application for merchant account management and reporting
- **Payment Processing Engine**: Core transaction processing, fraud detection, and settlement logic
- **Webhook Service**: Sends real-time payment notifications to merchant systems
- **Data Warehouse**: Stores transaction history and analytics data

**Infrastructure Design:**
- Multi-tier VPC architecture with public, private, and database subnets
- Application Load Balancers in public subnets
- EC2 instances for API and processing logic in private subnets
- Amazon RDS for transactional data (PostgreSQL Multi-AZ)
- Amazon Redshift for analytics and reporting
- AWS Lambda for webhook delivery
- ElastiCache for session management and rate limiting

**Expected Traffic:**
- 5,000 API requests per second at launch
- Sub-500ms p99 latency requirement
- 99.99% availability requirement (< 53 minutes downtime per year)

tabs.title: Requirements
tabs.content:
**Security Requirements (PCI DSS Compliance):**
- Network segmentation isolating cardholder data environment (CDE) from other systems
- All payment data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
- Implement principle of least privilege for all IAM roles and security groups
- Enable detailed logging of all access to payment data
- Deploy intrusion detection/prevention systems
- Implement DDoS protection for public endpoints
- Regular vulnerability scanning and penetration testing
- Multi-factor authentication for all administrative access

**Reliability Requirements:**
- Implement disaster recovery plan with RTO of 1 hour and RPO of 15 minutes
- Automated backups of all critical data with point-in-time recovery
- Geographic redundancy for payment processing (active-passive multi-region)
- Self-healing infrastructure that automatically replaces failed components
- Ability to perform zero-downtime deployments

**Cost Requirements:**
- Total infrastructure cost not to exceed $50,000/month in year 1
- Optimize compute costs using appropriate instance types and pricing models
- Implement cost allocation tags for different business units
- Forecast costs for 2x and 5x traffic growth scenarios
- Achieve at least 40% cost savings compared to on-premises alternative
